claim_summary:
Section 01 — Price distributions per product/day.

Mid mean / std / skew / kurtosis / quantiles + Jarque-Bera normality test.
raw_excerpt:
"""Section 01 — Price distributions per product/day.

Mid mean / std / skew / kurtosis / quantiles + Jarque-Bera normality test.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import pandas as pd
from scipy import stats as sps
from _common import (fmt_section_header, per_product_per_day_records, save_outputs)


def metric(p: str, d: int, sub: pd.DataFrame) -> dict:
    m = sub["mid"].astype(float)
    if len(m) < 8:
        return None
    arr = m.to_numpy()
    jb_stat, jb_p = sps.jarque_bera(arr)
    q = m.quantile([0.01, 0.05, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 0.95, 0.99])
    return {
        "n": int(len(m)),
        "mean": float(m.mean()),
        "std": float(m.std()),
        "min": float(m.min()),
        "max": float(m.max()),
        "skew": float(sps.skew(arr)),
        "kurt": float(sps.kurtosis(arr
